data.title: ARB price forecast aims for $0.23-$0.40 rebound in Q1 2025, amid mixed signals. Bearish sentiment prevails.
data.description: Forecasts for ARB's price anticipate a possible bounce back to the range of $0.23 to $0.40 in the next 1 to 4 weeks. This projection comes even amidst prevalent bearish sentiment and the highly fearful market environment.

data.title: Stellar (XLM) could surge to $0.30 within a month on bullish momentum, despite consolidating near $0.22 support.
data.description: Stellar Lumens (XLM) is on track to reach the $0.30 mark in the next 4-6 weeks according to price predictions. Despite facing consolidation around the crucial support level of $0.22, Stellar is displaying a bullish MACD divergence. This signals a potential upward trend in the near future.

data.title: Stonepeak to acquire majority interest in Castrol from bp for $10.1 billion, while bp retains a 35% minority stake.
data.description: Stonepeak, a major alternative investment firm focusing on infrastructure and real assets, has revealed a deal to purchase a majority controlling stake in Castrol, a renowned lubricant company, from BP p.l.c. The transaction values Castrol at an enterprise value worth about $10.1 billion. As part of the agreement, BP will maintain a 35% minority interest in Castrol.

data.description: Forecasts indicate a possible increase of 14-25% in LTC price, reaching a range of $87-95, contingent upon the maintenance of a support level at $74.66. Technical indicators portray a sense of careful hopefulness for Litecoin's short-term outlook.

data.title: Sanofi to buy Dynavax for $15.50 per share, a 39% premium. Adds hepatitis B vaccine and shingles drug to pipeline.
data.description: The stockholders of Dynavax will be receiving $15.50 in cash per share as part of an acquisition deal by Sanofi. This offer represents a 39% premium compared to Dynavax's closing share price on December 23, 2025. The agreement between Sanofi and Dynavax Technologies Corporation (Nasdaq: DVAX) was made public on December 24, 2025, in Emeryville, California.

data.title: Citius Pharmaceuticals (CTXR) stock surged 22.12% after hours to $1.27, following the launch of oncology therapy LYMPHIR for CTCL.
data.description: Citius Pharmaceuticals Inc. (NASDAQ: CTXR) saw a 22.12% surge in after-hours trading, reaching $1.27 on Tuesday following the company's fiscal year 2025 business update and the launch of its oncology therapy LYMPHIR. Marketed through its subsidiary Citius Oncology (NASDAQ: CTOR), LYMPHIR, an FDA-approved recombinant fusion protein, is now available commercially as of December. It targets adult patients with relapsed or refractory Stage I-III cutaneous T-cell lymphoma (CTCL) who have had at least one prior systemic treatment. Leonard Mazur, Chairman and CEO of Citius Pharmaceuticals, highlighted the significance of this milestone in the Tuesday press release.

data.title: Cosmos price predicted to hit $1.82 downside by Jan 2025 amid bearish trend despite brief bullish signals.
data.description: The forecast for the price of ATOM indicates ongoing weakness, with technical analysis indicating a potential downside target of $1.82. Despite some minor bullish signals from the MACD indicator, the overall outlook for Cosmos remains bearish.
